prompt: Looking straight up from the base of a massive ancient tree, wide angle lens, pure upward perspective. The trunk at the very bottom of the frame is single and whole. It splits into two, then four, then eight, then countless branches spreading across the entire sky above. The canopy is an overwhelming explosion of connections — every branch splits into more branches above it, endlessly multiplying outward. The sky is pale grey-white behind the dark sharp branches. High contrast. The viewer is at the bottom — the single point — looking up at an ever-expanding network of connections they can never match. Photorealistic, cinematic, 35mm wide angle, looking straight up. No text, no labels. The mood: vertiginous, inescapable, quietly uncanny.
visual_logic: A single trunk viewed from below splits into exponentially more branches upward — every node in the network has more connections above it than below, mathematically inevitable, inescapable from any vantage point.
